characters.physical_details: Occupies Dominic Altieri's 31-year-old body from Chapter 1: 5'10", dark brown hair, crooked nose, medium build, scar across the left knuckle. Nate's original 2025 body was thinner and wore glasses.

key_relationships.gail_ruiz: diner owner and lunch regular contact

characters.occupation_status: 2025: divorced remote tech-support worker with no children. 2005 as Dominic: Project Coordinator at Kassos Development.
characters.literacy_languages: English. Technically literate, but has no access to Dominic's memories.

state_transitions.change: Overwrites Dominic at 3:47 AM on July 11, 2005.
Learns Dominic had tried to protect Eastbridge tenants, including Mort.
Drains Dominic's checking and savings to write Tommy a 3,000-dollar check.
Signs the Eastbridge final closeout packet.
Takes a left-hand threaded screw from the Lazar's Hardware rubble.
Starts routing small jobs and customers to Mort's garage operation and keeps showing up weekly.

characters.physical_details: 5'10", dark hair, crooked nose, medium build, scar across left knuckle. Present only through the body Nate wears, his mail, and memory.

key_relationships.sarah_chen: live-in boyfriend of about four years
key_relationships.mort_lazar: Eastbridge tenant Dominic argued to protect

characters.occupation_status: Project Coordinator at Kassos Development, handling tenant communication, permits, and project follow-through.
characters.literacy_languages: English. Fluent in development paperwork and office politics.

state_transitions.change: Overwritten by Nate and ceases to exist as an active person.

characters.physical_details: Chinese-American history teacher with dark hair, practical clothes, pens, papers, and school routines always in reach.

key_relationships.nate_lazar: believes he is Dominic at first; later knows he is profoundly changed

characters.occupation_status: Bridgeport high-school history teacher; teaches summer school in July and returns to full school-year work after winter break.

state_transitions.change: Reads 'Dom' as softer and more present, even as the first shared-memory gap appears.
Moves from mood-based concern to factual suspicion after Marco, Cape Cod, and client-memory failures.
Confronts him directly and sleeps on the couch.
Stops testing old Dominic memories and relates to the changed man in front of her.

characters.physical_details: Greek-American, silver-gray hair, tanned, well-tailored, physically controlled rather than large.

key_relationships.dominic_altieri: useful protege who handled people well

characters.occupation_status: Owner of Kassos Development and a node in the lender-contractor-council network behind Eastbridge.

state_transitions.change: Starts treating Dominic as a competence problem.
Explains full-clearance financing, Dreher's network, and year-end draws lock Eastbridge, then warns Nate to stop pushing.
Requires signed closeout and makes execution the condition of continued employment.

characters.availability: Chapters 4, 9, 14, and 16, plus work-plot pressure throughout.

characters.physical_details: Reading glasses on a chain, canvas apron, rough scarred hands, and a good wool coat reserved for serious days.

key_relationships.nate_lazar: son, unknown to him; knows him only as Dom
key_relationships.dominic_altieri: name on the notice letter and face Nate now wears

characters.occupation_status: Owner of Lazar's Hardware, Unit 7 at Eastbridge Plaza, tenant since 1989. After demolition he sells stock from his garage.
characters.literacy_languages: English. Deeply fluent in hardware and repair work.

state_transitions.change: First real conversation with Dom at Gail's; explains the June notice letter.
Lets Dom into the store and accepts the offer to ask around.
Watches the store demolished.
Runs the garage operation and begins the decline Nate remembers leading into March 2006.

characters.physical_details: Small, practical, short-haired, usually carrying inventory or paperwork, often with a pencil behind one ear.

key_relationships.mort_lazar: wife, partner, and active watcher of his decline

characters.occupation_status: Co-owner, inventory manager, and bookkeeper for Lazar's Hardware; later helps run the garage setup.

state_transitions.change: Hears Dom promise to ask around and does not stop it.
Cries at the demolition and stays physically close to Mort.
Keeps the garage stock organized and stays within earshot of Mort.

characters.physical_details: Short, solid, energetic, usually in an apron with flour somewhere on her arms.

key_relationships.nate_lazar: believes he is Dominic and reads him as calmer than before
key_relationships.sarah_chen: warm but complicated after the bakery fight

characters.occupation_status: Runs a Bridgeport bakery with very early hours.

state_transitions.change: Reads Nate's altered behavior as a positive change in Dominic.
Keeps Sunday family routine while damp weather starts bothering her knee.

characters.availability: On-page in Chapters 6 and 18; active by phone and Sunday routine throughout.

characters.physical_details: Thinner and quicker than Dominic, with charm layered over panic; associated with a gold pinky ring and later a Saint Christopher medal.

key_relationships.nate_lazar: turns to him first for rescue money
key_relationships.renata_altieri: mother whose house he keeps endangering
key_relationships.sarah_chen: she reads his fear better than his performance

characters.occupation_status: Financially unstable gambler with vague day jobs and recurring debt trouble.

state_transitions.change: Asks for 1,500 dollars and gets 600.
Debt rises to 4,000 dollars after violence behind O'Rourke's; Nate gives him 3,000.

characters.physical_details: Tall, broad-shouldered, slightly soft, habitually tired, and fully built for office triage.

key_relationships.victor_kassos: boss whose judgment he tries not to test

characters.literacy_languages: English. Fluent in permits, sequencing, and lender constraints.

state_transitions.change: Loyalty strains after Nate's failed Eastbridge pitch.
Catches Nate blanking on Stu Halpern and says he cannot cover that forever.
Helps Nate sharpen a numbers-first pitch but warns him to let Victor's answer stand.

characters.physical_details: Fast, sturdy, mid-50s, hair pinned up, diner shoes flattened at the heel.

key_relationships.nate_lazar: lunch regular she knows as Dom

characters.occupation_status: Owner of Gail's Diner at Eastbridge Plaza; later reopens in a much smaller storefront two blocks west.

state_transitions.change: Becomes Nate's regular lunch counter and bridge into the Eastbridge community.
Walks away when the diner sign falls.
Keeps the smaller diner alive through routine and carryover customers.

characters.availability: Chapters 8, 17, and 18, plus off-page network presence.

timeline.story_start: July 11, 2005, 3:47 AM, in Dominic Altieri's apartment in Bridgeport, after a 2025 opening section in Nate's own apartment.
timeline.estimated_span: Roughly seven months on page: July 2005 through the first week of February 2006, with March 2006 still looming in Nate's memory as Mort's original-timeline death window.

seasonal_track.season: Late summer to mid-fall 2005: August through October.
Late fall to early winter 2005: late October through early December.

key_events.event: Nate first sees Mort alive through Lazar's Hardware.
Nate returns to Kassos and discovers Eastbridge is on Dominic's desk.
Nate and Sarah have sex for the first time after the overwrite.
Sunday dinner; Tommy asks for 1,500 dollars and gets 600.
Nate reads the full Eastbridge file and sees December 28 on paper.
Mort sits beside Nate at Gail's and explains the June notice letter.
Victor dismantles Nate's first inside-the-company delay pitch.
Marco visit, Cape Cod failure, Stu Halpern blank, and the spiral notebook.
Nate discovers Dominic's March-June sent-mail chain opposing Eastbridge.
Sarah confronts Nate about the months-long pattern of wrongness.
Nate enters Lazar's Hardware, buys a level, and promises Mort he will ask around.
Victor explains why full-clearance financing and politics have already locked Eastbridge.
Tommy's debt jumps to 4,000 dollars and Nate empties the remaining household cushion.
Nate signs the Eastbridge final closeout packet.
Gail reopens, Mort operates from the garage, and Nate starts routing small jobs to him.

key_locations.name: Dominic and Sarah's apartment
key_locations.details: One-bedroom mid-rise rental Dominic has occupied for nearly three years. Key continuity items: percolator, wall phone, Windows XP desktop, DVR, rattling summer AC, and overactive winter radiator.

key_locations.name: Kassos Development office
key_locations.details: Second floor of a converted brick warehouse with an open bullpen, Victor's office, a conference room, and a plotter room.

key_locations.name: Eastbridge Plaza
key_locations.details: Aging strip mall slated for mixed-use redevelopment: retail below apartments. Businesses named on-page are Nail Design by Kim, Park Cleaners, Gail's Diner, Al's Barbershop, Cesar's Market, and Lazar's Hardware.

key_locations.name: Lazar's Hardware
key_locations.details: Unit 7 end unit, tenant site since 1989, known for the specialty fasteners wall and the faded hand-painted sign.

key_locations.name: Altieri family house
key_locations.details: Small Bridgeport colonial with crowded family photos, a packed kitchen, and bakery spillover in the household routine.

key_locations.name: Gail's reopened diner
key_locations.details: Smaller storefront two blocks west of the old plaza, with six stools and tighter booths.

key_locations.name: Mort's garage
key_locations.details: Post-demolition sales space with salvaged shelves, labeled bins, a brass service bell, a space heater, and the old sign turned face-in.

distances: Lazar's Hardware is the larger end unit at Eastbridge Plaza.
Gail's Diner sat between Park Cleaners and Al's Barbershop in the old plaza.
Gail's reopened diner is two blocks west of the former Eastbridge site.
The Altieri house sits across Bridgeport from Dominic and Sarah's apartment.

world_rules: The machine performs a consciousness-only overwrite on an occupied biological target; Dominic does not remain inside the body.
Nate cannot simply go back to 2025 after Chapter 1.
Nate keeps his own memories and gains none of Dominic's.
Dominic's body retains practiced habits Nate does not consciously own: driving, signatures, kitchen movements, and reflexive intimacy.
Fourteen-year-old Nate Lazar still exists elsewhere in Bridgeport in 2005.
2005 constraints matter: no smartphones, no GPS, no cloud memory, and work life runs through landlines, flip phones, paper files, Windows XP, and Outlook.

power_structure.over: Dominic/Nate, Ray, and the Kassos office
power_structure.mechanism: Employment, file control, and paternalized discipline.

power_structure.holder: Victor Kassos, lenders, investors, and Councilman Dreher's network
power_structure.mechanism: Full-clearance financing, investor commitments, year-end draws, rezoning approval, and the demolition package already committed on paper.
Council approval plus the already-awarded demo package through Dreher's family network.

power_structure.over: Tommy and, indirectly, Renata and Dominic

power_structure.mechanism: Maternal authority, food, guilt, and Sunday routine.
Years of intimate memory and domestic pattern recognition.

objects.name: Vibe-coded machine
objects.origin: Built from AI-generated instructions, consumer electronics parts, and custom Python code.
objects.status: Used in Chapter 1 and effectively gone after the overwrite.

objects.name: Dominic's flip phone
objects.origin: Standard 2005 mobile phone holding family and work routines.

objects.name: Dominic's wallet
objects.origin: License, cards, membership card, and 43 dollars cash at first inventory.
objects.status: Carried throughout; cash cushion elsewhere is gone by Chapter 15.

objects.name: Rand McNally Connecticut road atlas
objects.origin: Glove-compartment navigation for a pre-smartphone world.

objects.name: Dominic's Honda Civic
objects.origin: Daily car used for work, Eastbridge lunches, family visits, and demolition-day witness.

objects.name: Eastbridge Plaza project file
objects.origin: Manila file with rent rolls, plans, permits, schedules, and tenant correspondence.
objects.status: Lives on Dominic's desk until demolition is complete.

objects.name: Morton's notice letter
objects.origin: June 8, 2005 tenant notice to Morton Lazar, signed in blue ink by Dominic Altieri.

objects.name: Spiral notebook dossier
objects.origin: Cheap pharmacy notebook cataloging Dominic's family, friends, work, trips, and mistakes.

objects.name: Dominic's Eastbridge sent-mail chain
objects.origin: March-June 2005 emails arguing for phased demolition, relocation help, and more time for named tenants.
objects.status: Discovered in Chapter 11 and proves Dominic fought the project internally.

objects.name: Twenty-four-inch level
objects.origin: Bought in person from Lazar's Hardware for Sarah's crooked shelf.
objects.status: Kept in the closet as a reminder of the promise to Mort.

objects.name: Eastbridge final site closeout packet
objects.origin: Packet containing site photos, utility disconnects, contractor release, and signature page.
objects.status: Signed by Nate as Dominic in Chapter 16.

objects.name: Left-hand threaded machine screw
objects.origin: Recovered from the Lazar's Hardware rubble; the kind of odd stock Mort kept when other stores stopped bothering.

debts.deadline: One week after the O'Rourke's assault
debts.status: Nate pays 3,000 dollars from Dominic's checking and savings; Tommy says more is still owed.

income_sources.reliability: Active but threatened if Nate stops executing.
Stable enough to cover groceries and the electric for a while after Chapter 15.

income_sources.amount: 2,200 dollars checking plus 800 dollars savings before Chapter 15; zero after Tommy's 3,000-dollar check clears
Small cash sales of leftover stock

knowledge_state.knows: He overwrote Dominic and cannot simply go back to 2025.
In the original timeline Mort is dead by March 2006, so the garage period is a countdown.
Eastbridge demolition is scheduled for December 28, 2005 and already broken into administrative steps.
Dominic spent March-June 2005 proposing delayed notices, relocation help, and phase-one preservation for Mort, Gail, and Mrs. Park.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Sarah, Mort, Linda, Gail, Tommy, and Renata.

knowledge_state.knows: Eastbridge is locked by full-clearance equity, lenders, investors, Dreher's network, year-end draws, and the already-awarded demo package.
The man in her apartment has been fundamentally wrong since July: missing kitchen memory, missing shared history, altered habits, and a pause before hearing 'Dom'.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: She still does not know the cause.

knowledge_state.knows: Dominic is missing client and file knowledge he should know cold and may be drifting toward a breakdown.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Ray reports enough upward to protect the project, but not a cause he does not know.

knowledge_state.knows: A man named Dom who works in real estate drank coffee at Gail's, entered the store, and promised to ask around about the redevelopment.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Mort does not know Dom is Nate or that Dom is the Dominic Altieri on the notice letter.

knowledge_state.knows: By Chapter 18, Dom from real estate is quietly steering odd jobs and customers toward Mort's garage operation.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Linda does not know Dom is Nate or why he is helping.

knowledge_state.knows: Dominic pushed back on Eastbridge in spring 2005 and, after Nate's arrival, has been getting personally entangled with Eastbridge tenants again.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Victor does not know why Dominic changed.

environmental_constants: Bridgeport summer is humid, sticky, and window-unit loud.
Lazar's Hardware smells of sawdust, cold metal, WD-40, and rubber mats.
Gail's Diner is coffee boiled too long, fryer grease, a tired bell, and a counter worn smooth by elbows.
Kassos Development is fluorescent hum, carpet tile, stale coffee, desktop fans, and paper everywhere.
The Altieri house is garlic, pork fat, bread, espresso, box fans, and too many family photos.
Dominic and Sarah's apartment has a rattling summer AC and clanking winter radiators.
After demolition, the old Eastbridge site becomes chain-link, green mesh, diesel, wet concrete, and a permit trailer.
Mort's garage smells of cold metal, damp cardboard, space-heater dust, and cooling coffee.
